generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Provides real-time temperature readings from building infrastructure for environmental monitoring and control optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Detects occupancy patterns within building spaces to enable adaptive control of lighting, HVAC, and other systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance LightLevelSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors ambient and artificial light levels to support lighting control systems and energy optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightLevelDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyMeter : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures real-time energy consumption across building systems to enable predictive analytics and efficiency recommendations."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

instance PostOccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Specialized sensor used in BOREALIS project to collect post-occupancy data for reinforcement learning-based control policy refinement."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PostOccupancyDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time temperature data stream from IoT sensors normalized for synchronization with BIM model."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Normalized occupancy data stream enabling real-time synchronization with building control systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance LightLevelDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Standardized light level measurements for lighting control and energy optimization systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightLevelSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Aggregated energy consumption data from building systems for predictive analytics and optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyMeter
]

instance PostOccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy sensor data used by BOREALIS for reinforcement learning model training and control policy adaptation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PostOccupancySensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimEnrichedModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantic backbone containing geometric, spatial, and functional building data enriched with device-specific metadata for runtime capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Normalized and validated real-time streams from IoT sensors and BCS (temperature, occupancy, energy usage) synchronized with BIM model."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance RuntimeControlPolicies : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Automatically generated control policies including reinforcement learning models from BOREALIS for adaptive building operations."
    DTDFVocab:fromData PostOccupancyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ReinforcementLearningEnabler
]

instance DigitalTwinArtifacts : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Automatically generated runtime interfaces (Eclipse Hono/Ditto configurations, Unreal Engine visualizations) stored in service-oriented architecture."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RealTimeOperationalData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngineEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automates extraction of domain semantics from BIM models and generates runtime artifacts via model-to-model and model-to-text transformations."
    DTDFVocab:enables AutomatedCommissioningService, RuntimeConfigurationService
]

instance EclipseHonoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Provides protocol-agnostic IoT connectivity for real-time data exchange between physical assets and digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, BidirectionalSynchronizationService
]

instance EclipseDittoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Serves as digital twin registry for bidirectional communication, device management, and state synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceManagementService, StateSynchronizationService
]

instance UnrealEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Offers rich, interactive visualization for immersive monitoring and simulation of building operations."
    DTDFVocab:enables ImmersiveVisualizationService, ScenarioSimulationService
]

instance ReinforcementLearningEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enables adaptive control policies that evolve based on post-occupancy sensor data through BOREALIS framework."
    DTDFVocab:enables AdaptiveLightingControlService, EnergyOptimizationService
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Adjusts lighting levels based on real-time data and control logic from TwinLight project, integrated via bidirectional communication protocols."
]

instance HvacActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controls HVAC units with real-time adjustments based on environmental sensors and optimization algorithms."
]

instance AdaptiveShadingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Automated shading system adjusting based on light levels and occupancy patterns for energy efficiency."
]

instance BuildingController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Centralized controller managing multiple building systems with automated commissioning capabilities."
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides live visualization of operational states (energy usage, occupancy, environmental conditions) via Unreal Engine 5."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeOperationalInsight, EnergyConsumptionInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Uses machine learning models to forecast energy consumption, visual comfort, and system failures for proactive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyConsumptionForecastInsight, SystemFailurePredictionInsight
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides data-driven suggestions for control policy adjustments to improve energy efficiency and occupant comfort."
    DTDFVocab:provides LightingScheduleOptimizationAction, HvacSetpointAdjustmentAction
]

instance ScenarioSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables what-if analysis for testing design changes or occupancy patterns before physical implementation."
    DTDFVocab:provides DesignImpactInsight
]

instance AutomatedCommissioningService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automatically configures building control systems via model-driven transformations from BIM to runtime artifacts."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviceConfigurationAction
]

instance BidirectionalSynchronizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Maintains real-time synchronization between physical assets and digital twin using Eclipse Hono and Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:provides SynchronizationFidelityInsight
]

instance AdaptiveLightingControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Implements reinforcement learning-based lighting control policies that adapt to occupancy and environmental conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides LightingEnergyEfficiencyAction
]

instance DeviceManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Manages device lifecycle including registration, configuration, and state monitoring through Eclipse Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviceHealthInsight
]

instance RuntimeConfigurationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Allows runtime adjustments to control policies without full redeployment of digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:provides PolicyAdjustmentAction
]

instance ImmersiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides interactive 3D visualization of building operations and system states using Unreal Engine 5."
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingStateVisualizationInsight
]

instance EnergyOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Optimizes energy consumption across building systems using reinforcement learning and predictive analytics."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyRecommendationAction
]

instance StateSynchronizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Ensures coherent state synchronization between physical and virtual systems with ODTE metric evaluation."
    DTDFVocab:provides SynchronizationQualityInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeOperationalInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current operational states including temperature, occupancy, and system statuses visualized in real-time."
]

instance EnergyConsumptionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detailed energy consumption metrics across building systems with historical trends and anomalies."
]

instance EnergyConsumptionForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive energy consumption estimates based on machine learning models and current operational data."
]

instance SystemFailurePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Anticipated system failures and maintenance requirements derived from predictive analytics."
]

instance LightingScheduleOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatically generated recommendations for optimal lighting schedules based on occupancy patterns."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance HvacSetpointAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Data-driven adjustments to HVAC setpoints for improved energy efficiency and comfort."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DeviceConfigurationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated commissioning and configuration of building control devices based on BIM model."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DesignImpactInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulated impact of design changes or occupancy patterns before physical implementation."
]

instance LightingEnergyEfficiencyAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adaptive lighting control actions that optimize energy usage while maintaining visual comfort."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DeviceHealthInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current health status and operational metrics of all building control devices."
]

instance PolicyAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Runtime adjustments to control policies without requiring full system redeployment."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance BuildingStateVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization of current building state including system statuses and environmental conditions."
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Comprehensive recommendations for improving overall building energy efficiency."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SynchronizationFidelityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative measure of synchronization quality between physical and virtual systems using ODTE metric."
]

instance SynchronizationQualityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Evaluation of state synchronization coherence and interoperability across distributed digital twin components."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The interactions from the virtual world to the physical world involve **control signals and commands** generated by the digital twin and transmitted back to the **building control systems (BCS)** and actuators. These outputs include **adjustments to lighting levels, HVAC settings, and other automated systems** based on real-time analytics, predictive models, or user-defined policies. The framework supports **bidirectional communication** via **Eclipse Hono and Ditto**, ensuring low-latency execution of control actions. For example, in the **BOREALIS** project, the DT uses **reinforcement learning** to dynamically optimize lighting control, while **TwinLight** enables **user-centered control strategies** for adaptive lighting systems."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale is **real-time or near-real-time**, with synchronization frequencies dependent on the **criticality of the data and control requirements**. For example:
  - **Sensor data** (e.g., occupancy, environmental conditions) is typically streamed at **high frequencies** (e.g., every few seconds to minutes) to ensure up-to-date monitoring.
  - **Control signals** (e.g., lighting adjustments, HVAC commands) are executed with **low latency** to maintain operational responsiveness.
  - **Long-term optimization** (e.g., reinforcement learning-based policies in BOREALIS) operates on **hourly or daily time scales** to refine control strategies iteratively.
  The framework supports **event-driven synchronization** where critical updates trigger immediate data exchange, while less time-sensitive data may be batched for efficiency."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is structured as a **service-oriented, modular architecture** with the following key components:
  1. **BIM Core**: The **semantic foundation** containing enriched building models and device metadata.
  2. **Middleware Layer**: **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** handle **real-time data exchange**, **device management**, and **bidirectional synchronization**.
  3. **Runtime Services**: Include **control logic execution**, **predictive analytics**, and **visualization** (e.g., Unreal Engine 5).
  4. **Extension Points**: Standardized interfaces for **custom service integration**, enabling **scalable, domain-specific extensions** (e.g., energy optimization, fault detection).
  5. **User Interfaces**: Role-based access for **Building Control Engineers** (device modeling), **Facility Managers** (monitoring/control), and **DT Engineers** (service development).
  The constellation is designed for **interoperability** across heterogeneous systems and supports **both centralized and decentralized deployment** models."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin spans **multiple life-cycle stages** of the building, covering:
  - **Design Phase**: Leverages **BIM models** to represent the **as-designed** building, including geometric, spatial, and functional attributes.
  - **Construction Phase**: Supports **clash detection, simulation, and documentation** using enriched BIM models.
  - **Operation Phase**: Enables **real-time monitoring, predictive maintenance, and adaptive control** by integrating **runtime data** (e.g., sensor readings, occupancy patterns) with the BIM backbone. The DT evolves alongside the physical building, allowing for **continuous updates** (e.g., refurbishments, retrofits) via **model-driven transformations**.
  The framework ensures **long-term compatibility** with AECO standards (e.g., BIM) and supports **legacy system integration** for buildings with existing infrastructure."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) Metric**: Quantifies the **alignment between the digital twin and physical twin**, measuring **data synchronization accuracy**, **control signal fidelity**, and **semantic coherence**.
  - **Model Validation**: Enriched BIM models are validated for **semantic consistency** and **compliance with AECO standards** before deployment.
  - **Real-Time Synchronization**: **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** enforce **low-latency, bidirectional data exchange** to minimize drift between physical and virtual systems.
  - **Uncertainty Handling**: The framework accounts for **sensor inaccuracies**, **network delays**, and **model approximations** by incorporating **adaptive control policies** (e.g., reinforcement learning in BOREALIS) that iteratively refine performance.
  - **Verification and Validation (V&V)**: Includes **case studies** (e.g., Living Lab deployments) to evaluate **operational effectiveness** and **stakeholder satisfaction**."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical twin (PT) and digital twin (DT) is established via:
  - **Eclipse Hono**: Provides **protocol-agnostic IoT connectivity**, supporting **MQTT, AMQP, and HTTP** for **real-time data ingestion** from sensors and BCS.
  - **Eclipse Ditto**: Acts as a **digital twin registry**, managing **device twins** with **state synchronization**, **event handling**, and **bidirectional communication**.
  - **Network Protocols**: **MQTT** (for lightweight, high-frequency sensor data) and **HTTP/REST** (for configuration and control commands).
  - **Architecture**: A **service-oriented middleware layer** decouples physical devices from the DT, enabling **scalable, modular integration**. The framework supports **edge computing** for latency-sensitive applications (e.g., real-time lighting control)."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital twin is hosted and deployed using:
  - **Cloud or On-Premises Infrastructure**: Supports **scalable deployment** for both small-scale (e.g., single buildings) and large-scale (e.g., campus-wide) implementations.
  - **Containerization**: Likely leverages **Docker/Kubernetes** for **modular service deployment** and **scalability**.
  - **Unreal Engine 5**: Hosts **interactive visualization** components, which may run on **dedicated rendering servers** or **client devices** for immersive monitoring.
  - **Middleware Stack**: **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** are deployed as **microservices** within the DT constellation, ensuring **high availability** and **fault tolerance**.
  - **Standardized Extension Points**: Enable **plug-and-play integration** of third-party services (e.g., analytics, visualization tools) without requiring full redeployment."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration with external systems is enabled through:
  - **Standardized APIs**: Expose **DT services** (e.g., data streams, control commands) via **RESTful APIs** or **message brokers** (e.g., Eclipse Hono) for interoperability with **ERP systems**, **CMMS**, or **other DTs**.
  - **BIM Integration**: Seamless exchange of **model data** with **AECO tools** (e.g., Revit, ArchiCAD) via **IFC (Industry Foundation Classes)** or **custom schemas**.
  - **IoT Platforms**: Connects to **third-party IoT ecosystems** (e.g., Siemens MindSphere, AWS IoT) for **sensor data aggregation** and **device management**.
  - **Service-Oriented Architecture**: Allows **loose coupling** with external systems via **standardized extension points**, enabling **modular integration** without tight dependencies."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin adheres to the following **standards and frameworks**:
  - **Building Information Modeling (BIM)**: Uses **BIM as the semantic backbone**, ensuring compatibility with **AECO industry standards** (e.g., IFC for data exchange).
  - **IoT Connectivity**: Leverages **Eclipse Hono and Ditto**, which comply with **OASIS Context Broker Architecture** and **Eclipse IoT standards** for interoperability.
  - **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)**: Follows **OMG’s Model-Driven Architecture (MDA)** principles for **abstraction**, **automation**, and **reusability**.
  - **Digital Twin Standards**: Aligns with **ISO/IEC 23247 (Digital Twin Framework)** and **NIST’s Digital Twin Reference Architecture** for **modularity** and **scalability**.
  - **Visualization**: Uses **Unreal Engine 5**, which supports **open standards** for **3D modeling** and **interactive rendering**."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through:
  - **Authentication and Authorization**: **Role-based access control (RBAC)** for **Building Control Engineers**, **Facility Managers**, and **DT Engineers**, ensuring **least-privilege access** to DT services.
  - **Data Encryption**: **TLS/SSL** for secure **data transmission** between physical devices and the DT, and **end-to-end encryption** for sensitive operational data.
  - **Middleware Security**: **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** incorporate **OAuth 2.0** and **JWT** for **secure device authentication** and **API access control**.
  - **Safety Mechanisms**: **Fail-safes** in control logic (e.g., **hardware overrides**, **emergency shutdowns**) to prevent **unsafe operations** (e.g., lighting failures, HVAC malfunctions).
  - **Compliance**: Adheres to **ISO/IEC 27001 (Information Security Management)** and **NIST SP 800-53** for **cybersecurity best practices**.
  - **Remote Operation Safeguards**: **Human-in-the-loop validation** for critical control actions to mitigate risks of **unintended remote operations** affecting physical systems."
]
